name: context-bridge-vscode
description: Continue developer agent sessions across Claude, Codex, and other tools from VS Code.

commands.title: Context Bridge: Import Latest Claude Session
Context Bridge: Import Latest Codex Session
Context Bridge: Handoff to Existing Claude Session
Context Bridge: Handoff to New Claude Session
Context Bridge: Handoff to Existing Codex Session
Context Bridge: Handoff to New Codex Session
Context Bridge: Copy Latest Handoff Prompt

contextBridge.maxExportChars.description: Optional maximum characters to embed in generated handoffs. 0 means no deterministic clipping.

contextBridge.dedupeTurns.description: Collapse consecutive duplicate turns. Native logs (e.g. Codex) record each message under several event types, producing 2-3 identical copies; this removes the repeats.

contextBridge.toolMaxChars.description: Truncate tool-output turns longer than this many characters (keeping head and tail). 0 disables truncation. Tool outputs like git diffs and directory listings dominate handoff size.

contextBridge.systemMaxChars.description: Truncate system turns (e.g. repeated turn-context blobs) longer than this many characters. 0 disables truncation.

contextBridge.openHandoffDocument.description: Open the generated handoff markdown document after export.

contextBridge.claudeUri.description: Optional URI used to open or focus Claude Code when contextBridge.allowExternalClaudeUri is enabled.

contextBridge.allowExternalClaudeUri.description: Allow Context Bridge to open contextBridge.claudeUri. Keep false in VS Code forks so handoff stays in the current editor.

contextBridge.claudeOpenCommand.description: Optional VS Code command id used to open or focus Claude. If empty, Context Bridge tries to find a command containing 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

contextBridge.codexOpenCommand.description: Optional VS Code command id used to open or focus Codex. If empty, Context Bridge tries to find a command containing 'codex'.
